with superfluous phrases,

sentences.original: But in this case

sentences.translationRu: Её лицо поблекло и стало печальным.

and on both sides of it

Anna Sergeyevna was touching;

sentences.translationRu: В ней было что-то чистое, как у простой, хорошей женщины,
sentences.original: there was about her the purity of a good, simple woman

sentences.original: The solitary candle burning on the table

sentences.original: threw a faint light on her face,

"Forgiven? No.

sentences.original: I don't know what he does there, what his work is,

sentences.translationRu: но я знаю, что он лакей.
sentences.original: but I know he is a flunkey!

sentences.text: Tenía veinte años cuando me casé con él.
sentences.translationRu: Мне было двадцать лет, когда я вышла за него замуж.
sentences.original: I was twenty when I was married to him. I have been tormented by curiosity; I wanted something better. 'There must be a different sort of life,' I said to myself. I wanted to live! To live, to live!... I was fired by curiosity ...
you don't understand it, but, I swear to God, I could not control myself; something happened to me: I could not be restrained.
I told my husband I was ill, and came here....

sentences.text: «Debe de existir una vida diferente», me decía yo.
sentences.translationRu: «Должна быть другая жизнь», — говорила я себе.
sentences.original: And here I have been walking about as though I were dazed, like a mad creature; ...
and now I have become a vulgar,
contemptible woman whom any one may despise."
Gurov felt bored already, listening to her.
He was irritated by the naïve tone, by this remorse, so unexpected and inopportune;
but for the tears in her eyes, he might have thought she was jesting or playing a part.
"I don't understand," he said softly.

sentences.text: Le dije a mi marido que estaba enferma
sentences.original: "What is it you want?"

sentences.translationRu: И здесь я ходила как потерянная, как безумная.
sentences.original: She hid her face on his breast and pressed close to him.

sentences.text: Y ahora me he convertido en una mujer vulgar y despreciable
sentences.translationRu: А теперь я стала пошлой, презренной женщиной,
sentences.original: "Believe me, believe me, I beseech you ..." she said.

sentences.translationRu: Гуров уже чувствовал скуку, слушая её.
sentences.original: "I love a pure, honest life, and sin is loathsome to me.
I don't know what I am doing.

sentences.text: y este arrepentimiento tan inesperado e inoportuno.
sentences.translationRu: и это раскаяние, такое неожиданное и неуместное.

sentences.text: Si no fuera por las lágrimas en sus ojos,
sentences.translationRu: Если бы не слёзы на её глазах,

sentences.text: habría pensado que ella estaba bromeando o actuando.
sentences.translationRu: он мог бы подумать, что она шутит или притворяется.

sentences.original: And I may say of myself now that the Evil One has beguiled me."

sentences.translationRu: Она спрятала лицо у него на груди
sentences.original: "Hush, hush!..." he muttered.

sentences.translationRu: — Верь мне, верь мне, прошу тебя,
sentences.original: He looked at her fixed, scared eyes, kissed her, talked softly and affectionately,

sentences.text: — Amo una vida pura y honesta,
y el pecado me da asco.
No sé lo que estoy haciendo.
sentences.translationRu: Я не знаю, что со мной происходит.
sentences.original: and by degrees she was comforted, and her gaiety returned;
they both began laughing.

sentences.text: Y yo puedo decir lo mismo ahora:
sentences.translationRu: И я могу сказать то же самое сейчас:

sentences.original: Afterwards when they went out there was not a soul on the sea-front.

sentences.text: Él la miró a los ojos fijos y asustados.
sentences.translationRu: Он смотрел в её неподвижные, испуганные глаза.
sentences.original: The town with its cypresses had quite a deathlike air,
but the sea still broke noisily on the shore;

sentences.text: y habló con ella suave y tiernamente.
sentences.translationRu: и говорил с ней мягко и нежно.

sentences.text: Poco a poco ella se calmó

They found a cab and drove to Oreanda.
"I found out your surname in the hall just now: it was written on the board--Von Diderits," said Gurov.

sentences.text: no había nadie en el paseo marítimo.
sentences.translationRu: на набережной не было ни души.

sentences.translationRu: Город с его кипарисами выглядел совершенно мёртвым.
sentences.original: "Is your husband a German?"

sentences.original: "No; I believe his grandfather was a German, but he is an Orthodox Russian himself."

sentences.text: Una sola barca se balanceaba sobre las olas

sentences.text: y en ella parpadeaba dormilona una linterna.

sentences.text: — Encontré su apellido en el vestíbulo hace un momento.
sentences.translationRu: — Я только что узнал вашу фамилию в вестибюле.
sentences.original: white clouds stood motionless on the mountain-tops.

sentences.translationRu: В Ореанде они сели на скамейку недалеко от церкви
sentences.original: grasshoppers chirruped,

sentences.original: and the monotonous hollow sound of the sea rising up from below, spoke of the peace, of the eternal sleep awaiting us.

sentences.original: so it sounds now, and it will sound as indifferently and monotonously when we are all no more.
And in this constancy, in this complete indifference to the life and death of each of us, there lies hid, perhaps, a pledge of our eternal salvation,

sentences.translationRu: Монотонный, глухой звук моря, поднимавшийся снизу,
sentences.original: of the unceasing movement of life upon earth, of unceasing progress towards perfection.

sentences.text: hablaba de paz y del eterno sueño que nos espera.
sentences.translationRu: говорил о покое и о вечном сне, ожидающем нас.

sentences.original: Sitting beside a young woman who in the dawn seemed so lovely, soothed and spellbound in these magical surroundings--the sea, mountains, clouds, the open sky--Gurov thought how in reality everything is beautiful in this world when one reflects:
everything except what we think or do ourselves when we forget our human dignity and the higher aims of our existence.

sentences.text: y seguirá sonando con la misma indiferencia y monotonía

sentences.text: en esta indiferencia total ante la vida y la muerte de cada uno de nosotros,

sentences.text: y el avance continuo hacia la perfección.

sentences.translationRu: Гуров думал о том, что в действительности всё прекрасно на этом свете, когда задумываешься.

sentences.translationRu: Они увидели, как из Феодосии приближается пароход

sentences.original: lunched and dined together, went for walks, admired the sea.

sentences.text: — dijo Anna Serguéievna después de un silencio.

sentences.original: She complained that she slept badly, that her heart throbbed violently;

Complete idleness, these kisses in broad daylight while he looked round in dread of some one's seeing them, the heat, the smell of the sea, and the continual passing to and fro before him of idle, well-dressed, well-fed people, made a new man of him;

sentences.text: Ella se quejaba de que dormía mal

sentences.translationRu: и что сердце у неё сильно бьётся.

sentences.original: He was impatiently passionate,

sentences.translationRu: то страхом, что он недостаточно её уважает.

sentences.translationRu: И часто, на площади или в садах,

sentences.translationRu: он вдруг притягивал её к себе

sentences.text: el calor, el olor del mar,

sentences.translationRu: постоянное мелькание нарядных и сытых людей

sentences.translationRu: Не хотел отходить от неё ни на шаг.

sentences.text: Ella, en cambio, estaba a menudo pensativa

que no la respetaba, que no la amaba en absoluto

sentences.translationRu: и думает о ней только как об обычной женщине.

sentences.text: a Oreanda o a la cascada.

sentences.original: "It's the finger of destiny!"

Anna Serguéievna se apresuró a marcharse.

When she had got into a compartment of the express, and when the second bell had rung, she said:
"Let me look at you once more ... look at you once again. That's right."
She did not shed tears, but was so sad that she seemed ill, and her face was quivering.

sentences.text: Cuando ella entró en un compartimento del tren expreso
sentences.translationRu: Когда она вошла в купе скорого поезда

sentences.translationRu: — Дай мне посмотреть на тебя ещё раз...
sentences.original: "God be with you; be happy.

We are parting forever--it must be so,
for we ought never to have met.
Well, God be with you."
The train moved off rapidly, its lights soon vanished from sight,
and a minute later there was no sound of it, as though everything had conspired together to end as quickly as possible that sweet delirium, that madness.
Left alone on the platform, and gazing into the dark distance, Gurov listened to the chirrup of the grasshoppers and the hum of the telegraph wires,

sentences.text: como si todo se hubiera puesto de acuerdo
para poner fin lo más rápido posible

sentences.original: He was moved, sad, and conscious of a slight remorse.

sentences.translationRu: Он чувствовал себя так, словно только что проснулся.
